description: This figure presents a dual-axis line plot illustrating the compositional dependence of Poisson's ratio (δ) and the B/G ratio (bulk-to-shear modulus ratio, inverse of Pugh's modulus ratio) in the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl alloy system calculated via first-principles DFT. The x-axis represents Mn content (x) ranging from 0 (FeAl) to 1.0 (MnAl), while the left y-axis displays Poisson's ratio (red squares, δ) and the right y-axis shows B/G values (blue squares). Both curves exhibit nearly identical variation trends: they increase gradually from FeAl (Poisson's ratio ≈0.236, B/G≈1.56), reach a maximum at Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al (Poisson's ratio ≈0.309, B/G≈2.28), then decrease to a local minimum around Fe_{0.25}Mn_{0.75}Al before rising sharply toward MnAl (Poisson's ratio ≈0.324, B/G≈2.52). According to the Pugh criterion where B/G>1.75 indicates ductility, the figure reveals that FeAl and Fe_{0.875}Mn_{0.125}Al are brittle, while alloys with higher Mn content become ductile, with Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al possessing the optimal balance of hardness and ductility among the studied compositions.
